questions.q: In which 1987 Hindi science-fiction film did Sridevi play a journalist alongside Anil Kapoor and an invisible hero?
questions.explanation: Sridevi played the journalist Seema in Shekhar Kapur's 1987 film Mr. India, opposite Anil Kapoor.

questions.q: In which 1986 film did Sridevi play a shape-shifting serpent woman who could transform into a snake?
questions.explanation: Sridevi played the icchadhari naagin Rajni in the 1986 hit Nagina.

questions.q: Sridevi was a leading star of Hindi cinema during which decade?
questions.explanation: Sridevi became Hindi cinema's foremost female star of the 1980s with hits like Himmatwala, Nagina and Mr. India.

questions.q: Besides Hindi, in which two South Indian film industries did Sridevi work extensively?
questions.explanation: Sridevi worked across Tamil, Telugu and Hindi cinema during her career.

questions.q: In which 1989 Yash Chopra film did Sridevi play the title character opposite Rishi Kapoor and Vinod Khanna?
questions.explanation: Sridevi played the title role in Yash Chopra's 1989 romance Chandni.

questions.q: In which 1983 film starring Sridevi and Jeetendra did she perform to the popular song 'Taki O Taki'?
questions.explanation: The song 'Taki O Taki' is from the 1983 blockbuster Himmatwala, which paired Sridevi with Jeetendra.

questions.q: After a long break from films, in which 2012 film did Sridevi make her acting comeback as a housewife who learns English?
questions.explanation: Sridevi returned to acting in Gauri Shinde's 2012 film English Vinglish, playing Shashi Godbole.

questions.q: Which life event involving Sridevi occurred in 2018?
questions.explanation: Sridevi died in February 2018 at the age of 54.

questions.q: Which actor co-starred with Sridevi as the invisible hero in the 1987 film Mr. India?
questions.explanation: Anil Kapoor played Arun, the man who becomes invisible, opposite Sridevi in Mr. India.

questions.q: Sridevi is widely described in Indian cinema as the first female what?
questions.explanation: Sridevi is often called Indian cinema's first female superstar for her box-office draw in the 1980s.

questions.q: In which year was Sridevi born?
questions.explanation: Sridevi was born in 1963 in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: In the 1983 film Sadma, which actor played the man who cares for Sridevi's character suffering from amnesia?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan starred opposite Sridevi in Sadma, the Hindi remake of the Tamil film Moondram Pirai.

questions.q: In the 1991 Yash Chopra film Lamhe, Sridevi played how many distinct roles?
options: A double role of mother and daughter
She was only in a cameo
questions.explanation: In Lamhe (1991) Sridevi played a double role as Pallavi and her daughter Pooja.

questions.q: In the 1989 comedy ChaalBaaz, Sridevi played a double role of two sisters opposite which two heroes?
questions.explanation: In ChaalBaaz (1989) Sridevi played twin sisters Anju and Manju opposite Sunny Deol and Rajinikanth.

questions.q: Which civilian honour was conferred on Sridevi by the Government of India in 2013?
questions.explanation: Sridevi was awarded the Padma Shri, India's fourth-highest civilian honour, in 2013.

questions.q: Whom did Sridevi marry in 1996?
questions.explanation: Sridevi married film producer Boney Kapoor in 1996.

questions.q: In the 1986 film Nagina, the snake charmer who battles Sridevi's naagin character is played by which actor?
questions.explanation: Amrish Puri played the menacing snake charmer Bhairo Nath in Nagina (1986).

questions.q: The famous song 'Hawa Hawai', picturised on Sridevi, is from which of her films?
questions.explanation: 'Hawa Hawai', composed by Laxmikant-Pyarelal, was picturised on Sridevi in Mr. India (1987).

questions.q: Sridevi played one of her last leading roles as a grieving mother seeking justice in which 2017 film?
questions.explanation: Sridevi starred as Devki Sabarwal in the 2017 thriller Mom, one of her final films.

questions.q: Sridevi reunited with Jeetendra for which 1984 hit that also won her acclaim alongside Jaya Prada?
questions.explanation: Tohfa (1984), pairing Sridevi with Jeetendra and Jaya Prada, was one of the biggest hits of the year.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Sridevi born?
questions.explanation: Sridevi was born in Sivakasi in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Sridevi's National Film Award for Best Actress, received posthumously, was for which film?
questions.explanation: Sridevi was posthumously awarded the National Film Award for Best Actress for Mom (2017).

questions.q: The 1983 film Sadma was a Hindi remake of which Tamil film, also starring Sridevi and Kamal Haasan?
questions.explanation: Sadma (1983) was the Hindi remake of the Tamil film Moondram Pirai, which also featured Sridevi and Kamal Haasan.

questions.q: Sridevi appeared with Kamal Haasan and Rajinikanth in which acclaimed 1977 Tamil film that marked Bharathiraja's directorial debut?
questions.explanation: 16 Vayathinile (1977), Bharathiraja's debut as director, starred Sridevi alongside Kamal Haasan and Rajinikanth and was a major Tamil hit.

questions.q: Which composer scored Sridevi's Jeetendra-starrer blockbusters Himmatwala (1983) and Tohfa (1984)?
questions.explanation: Bappi Lahiri composed the music for the Sridevi-Jeetendra hits Himmatwala (1983) and Tohfa (1984).

questions.q: Sridevi began acting as a child artiste; in roughly which year did she first appear on screen?
questions.explanation: Sridevi started as a child artiste in the mid-1960s, debuting around age four in the 1967 Tamil film Kandhan Karunai.

questions.q: In the 1997 film Judaai, Sridevi played a wife who effectively sells her husband to another woman; which actor played the husband?
questions.explanation: In Judaai (1997) Sridevi starred as Kajal opposite Anil Kapoor, with Urmila Matondkar as the other woman.

questions.q: Through hits such as the 1978 film Padaharella Vayasu, Sridevi became a major star in which South Indian film industry during the late 1970s and 1980s?
questions.explanation: Padaharella Vayasu (1978), the Telugu remake of 16 Vayathinile, relaunched Sridevi in Telugu cinema, where she became a leading star through the late 1970s and 1980s.

questions.q: In the 1993 film Gumrah, Sridevi played a woman wrongly imprisoned abroad on drug charges, co-starring which actor?
questions.explanation: In Gumrah (1993) Sridevi played Roshni opposite Sanjay Dutt.

questions.q: Sridevi's elder daughter, who later entered Hindi cinema as an actress, is named what?
questions.explanation: Janhvi Kapoor, Sridevi's elder daughter with Boney Kapoor, became a Hindi film actress.
